areas.name: Recreation Programming (General)
areas.context: CivicRec is the primary registration and program management system.

areas.name: Childcare and Out-of-School Time Enrichment
areas.context: The department operates both licensed and unlicensed childcare and out-of-school programs. Licensing status is an active compliance distinction.
key_considerations: Maryland childcare licensing requirements [VERIFY BEFORE PUBLISHING]

areas.name: Teens and Adventure Sports
areas.context: A growing program area involving off-site trips, physical risk activities, and specialized equipment.
key_considerations: Risk management thresholds for adventure activities [VERIFY BEFORE PUBLISHING — confirm with City Risk Management]

areas.name: Special Events
areas.context: The division plans, supports, and licenses community events and ceremonies each year. This includes both department-produced events (Hometown Holidays, Independence Day Celebration, Antique and Classic Car Show, weekly Farmers Market) and licensed third-party events in parks and facilities.
key_considerations: City event permit requirements [VERIFY BEFORE PUBLISHING]
ADA access plan per event site
areas.format_note: Event safety briefings and staff communication protocols for Special Events are Format Type 4 (Communication Protocol), not Format Type 1.

areas.name: Farmers Market and Eat Fresh Program
key_roles: Program coordination with Capital Area Food Bank for Mobile Market
areas.context: The weekly Farmers Market is a department-produced event. The Eat Fresh program distributes produce vouchers to low-income families using SNAP/EBT benefits.
key_considerations: USDA SNAP program requirements [VERIFY BEFORE PUBLISHING]
Food safety handling standards [VERIFY BEFORE PUBLISHING]

areas.name: Recreation Fund (Financial Assistance)
areas.context: The Recreation Fund collects donations and provides financial assistance to eligible residents for program participation.
key_considerations: Eligibility criteria [VERIFY BEFORE PUBLISHING — confirm with Program Access Supervisor]
Reporting to the Recreation and Park Advisory Board
